name: Extreme Sports
description: Skateboarding, surfing, BASE jumping, and other action sports

questions.q: Tony Hawk is widely considered the face of which extreme sport?

source.notes: Turned pro at 14; revolutionized vert skating in the 80s and 90s.

questions.q: What does 'BMX', the freestyle and dirt bike sport, stand for?

source.notes: Emerged in 1970s Southern California on 20-inch bikes.

questions.q: Which action-sports competition series is organized each year by ESPN?

source.notes: Launched 1995; split into Summer X Games and Winter X Games.

questions.q: Which American daredevil was famous in the 1970s for jumping motorcycles over long rows of cars, buses, and Snake River Canyon?

source.notes: Born Robert Knievel; suffered hundreds of bone fractures over his career.

questions.q: Which extreme sport involves parachuting or wingsuit-flying from a fixed object like a cliff or tower?

source.notes: BASE = Building, Antenna, Span, Earth.

questions.q: American Kelly Slater is widely considered the greatest of all time in which extreme sport?

source.notes: 11-time World Surf League champion.

questions.q: What is the U-shaped structure used in skateboarding, BMX, and snowboarding called?

source.notes: Originally modeled after empty swimming pools that skaters rode in 1970s California.

questions.q: In what year did Tony Hawk famously become the first skateboarder to land a '900' (two and a half mid-air spins)?

source.notes: Landed at X Games V on June 27, 1999 in San Francisco.

questions.q: Commercial bungee jumping was pioneered in 1988 with operations in which country?

source.notes: A.J. Hackett opened the first commercial operation at Kawarau Bridge, Queenstown.

questions.q: Which Hawaiian swimmer and surfer is credited with popularizing surfing around the world in the early 20th century?

source.notes: Olympic swim gold medallist; credited with reviving surfing and taking it global.

questions.q: What is a 'wipeout' in surfing slang?

source.notes: Also the title of The Surfaris' 1963 instrumental hit.

questions.q: Free-soloing is the extreme form of which sport, performed without ropes or safety gear?

source.notes: Made famous by climber Alex Honnold's free-solo ascent of El Capitan.

questions.q: In 2017, Alex Honnold became the first person to free-solo which famous 3,000-foot granite rock wall in Yosemite?

source.notes: Documented in the 2018 Oscar-winning film 'Free Solo'.

questions.q: The professional motorcycle road-racing championship held each year across Europe, Asia, and the Americas is called what?

source.notes: Top class uses 1000cc prototype machines.

questions.q: Swiss-born Xavier de Le Rue, Travis Rice, and Jeremy Jones are leading figures in which extreme sport?

source.notes: All known for freeriding in steep, avalanche-prone terrain.

questions.q: The annual surfing competition 'Billabong Pipe Masters' takes place at the Banzai Pipeline on which Hawaiian island?

source.notes: On the North Shore of Oahu; held each December.

questions.q: Bethany Hamilton famously lost her arm to what animal while surfing in 2003?

source.notes: Tiger shark attack off Kauai; returned to competitive surfing within a year.

questions.q: The Dakar Rally, one of the world's toughest off-road races, originally ran from Paris to which African city?

source.notes: Dakar, Senegal; now held in Saudi Arabia since 2020.

questions.q: In 2012 Austrian Felix Baumgartner broke the speed of sound in freefall during which Red Bull-backed jump?

source.notes: Jumped from about 38.97 km on October 14, 2012.

questions.q: The acronym 'BASE' in BASE jumping stands for which four types of fixed objects?

source.notes: Term coined by filmmaker Carl Boenish in 1978.

questions.q: Which New Zealander is widely credited with turning bungee jumping into a commercial sport in the late 1980s?

source.notes: First commercial jump at the 43-metre Kawarau Gorge Suspension Bridge in November 1988.

questions.q: The legendary 'Big Wave' surf spot Jaws (or 'Pe'ahi') is located off which Hawaiian island?

source.notes: Winter swells can reach 20m+; ridden via tow-in since the mid-1990s.
